Cross-platform availability including iOS, Android, and desktop

name: agency-agents
description: A complete AI agency at your fingertips - From frontend wizards to Reddit community ninjas, from whimsy injectors to reality checkers. Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables.

curation.summary: The Agency is a curated collection of specialized AI agent personas — each with distinct expertise, personality, and defined workflows — designed to be installed into AI coding tools like Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, and others. It provides a standardized roster of 'specialist' agents (e.g., frontend wizards, community managers, reality checkers) as markdown-based agent definitions with shell installation scripts and a companion native desktop app.
curation.whyNotable: With over 123K stars and a companion native app, this project has tapped into a massive demand for specialized AI agent personas that work across multiple AI coding tools. Its cross-platform compatibility and curated, personality-driven approach to AI agent definitions make it a practical resource for anyone looking to enhance their AI-assisted workflows without building agents from scratch.
strengths: Cross-tool compatibility supporting Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, and more with a single agent definition set
Well-curated, personality-driven agent personas with defined workflows and deliverables rather than generic prompt templates
Strong community adoption with 123K+ stars, 87 contributors, and a native desktop app for frictionless installation
Excellent README with clear quick-start instructions, badges, and visual structure
limitations: Fundamentally a curated prompt/persona library rather than a functional agent framework — relies entirely on external AI tools to operate
No tests or quality validation mechanisms for measuring agent effectiveness or consistency across updates
Shell-based project with no Docker support limits reproducibility and deployment flexibility
useCases: Enhancing AI coding assistants with specialized expert personas for domains like frontend development, community management, or QA
Teams wanting to standardize agent behavior across multiple AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, etc.)
Developers looking for ready-made agent personalities to reduce prompt engineering effort in their AI workflows
curation.targetAudience: Developers and teams using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini) who want to quickly deploy specialized agent personas without crafting their own system prompts
curation.oneLiner: A massively popular curated roster of personality-driven AI agent definitions installable across all major AI coding tools with one click

name: cupy
description: NumPy & SciPy for GPU

curation.summary: CuPy is a GPU-accelerated array library that serves as a drop-in replacement for NumPy and SciPy, enabling high-performance computing on NVIDIA CUDA and AMD ROCm platforms. It provides a familiar API for numerical computing while leveraging GPU parallelism for significant speedups.
curation.whyNotable: CuPy is a mature, widely-adopted project that bridges the gap between CPU-based scientific computing and GPU acceleration, with extensive compatibility with NumPy/SciPy APIs and support for both CUDA and ROCm backends.
strengths: Drop-in replacement for NumPy/SciPy with high API compatibility
Supports both NVIDIA CUDA and AMD ROCm platforms
Extensive documentation, tutorials, and examples
Active development with frequent releases and large community
limitations: Installation can be complex due to CUDA/ROCm version dependencies
Not all NumPy/SciPy functions are implemented yet
Performance gains depend on problem size and GPU memory availability
useCases: Accelerating numerical computations in scientific research
Deep learning data preprocessing and custom operations
High-performance computing tasks requiring GPU parallelism
curation.targetAudience: Data scientists, researchers, and engineers working with large-scale numerical computations who need GPU acceleration without leaving the Python ecosystem.
curation.oneLiner: CuPy brings NumPy/SciPy to the GPU with drop-in compatibility and multi-platform support.

name: FluidVoice
description: Fastest and only macOS Dictation app with on-device STT and custom trained AI enhancement model - Local Wispr Flow alternative. One ⭐ takes us a long way :)) Windows, iOS and Linux coming soon.

curation.summary: FluidVoice is an open-source macOS dictation application that provides fast, fully on-device speech-to-text using models like Parakeet, Whisper, and Nemotron, combined with a proprietary 'Fluid Intelligence' AI enhancement model for smart formatting and post-processing. It's positioned as a local, privacy-first alternative to cloud-based dictation tools like Wispr Flow, with no data leaving the user's Mac.
curation.whyNotable: One of the few open-source, on-device dictation apps achieving real-time performance with local AI enhancement — no cloud APIs required. Its rapid release cadence (17 releases in ~9 months), strong community adoption (5,500+ stars), and Homebrew distribution make it unusually polished for an open-source desktop AI application.
strengths: Fully on-device inference with no cloud dependency, strong privacy proposition
Impressive release velocity and active development with 17 releases in 6 months
Clean distribution via Homebrew cask, making installation trivially easy for macOS users
Supports multiple STT backends (Parakeet, Whisper, Nemotron, Apple Speech) giving users flexibility
Strong community traction with 5,500+ stars and growing
limitations: No automated test suite, raising concerns about regression safety and code quality assurance
The 'Fluid Intelligence' AI enhancement component is closed-source/proprietary despite the GPL license, creating an unusual open-core tension
Limited to macOS currently — iOS, Windows, and Linux support announced but not yet available
useCases: Writers and professionals who need fast, accurate dictation without cloud privacy concerns
Users seeking a free, open-source alternative to Wispr Flow, Dragon, or similar paid dictation tools
Developers who want a local dictation engine to integrate or extend for their own workflows
curation.targetAudience: Mac users who need frequent voice-to-text dictation — writers, developers, accessibility users, and anyone prioritizing privacy by keeping speech data on-device
curation.oneLiner: A blazing-fast, privacy-first macOS dictation app with on-device AI enhancement — the open-source local alternative to Wispr Flow

name: logto
description: 🧑‍🚀 Authentication and authorization infrastructure for SaaS and AI apps, built on OIDC and OAuth 2.1 with multi-tenancy, SSO, and RBAC.

curation.summary: Logto is an open-source authentication and authorization platform built on OIDC and OAuth 2.1, designed for SaaS and AI applications. It provides multi-tenancy, enterprise SSO, RBAC, pre-built sign-in flows, and SDKs for 30+ frameworks, with specific support for Model Context Protocol and agent-based AI architectures.
curation.whyNotable: Logto stands out by positioning itself as auth infrastructure purpose-built for the AI era, with explicit support for MCP and agent-based architectures alongside traditional SaaS auth needs. With 13k+ stars, a cloud offering, and active development, it's one of the most popular open-source alternatives to Auth0 and Clerk.
strengths: Strong OIDC/OAuth 2.1 and SAML protocol support with multi-tenancy and enterprise SSO out-of-the-box
Large community adoption (13k+ stars, 105 contributors) with active releases and cloud offering
SDKs for 30+ frameworks and customizable UI components reduce integration effort significantly
limitations: Not primarily an AI-native tool—auth infrastructure that adds AI-app support rather than being AI-first
No examples directory listed and tests status shows false despite code coverage badges, suggesting inconsistent testing documentation
useCases: Securing multi-tenant SaaS applications with enterprise SSO and RBAC
Adding authentication to AI agent platforms and MCP-based architectures
Replacing hosted auth providers like Auth0 or Clerk with a self-hosted open-source alternative
curation.targetAudience: Backend and full-stack developers building SaaS products or AI-powered platforms who need production-grade auth without vendor lock-in
curation.oneLiner: Modern open-source auth infrastructure bridging traditional SaaS and AI-native app authentication with OIDC/OAuth 2.1, multi-tenancy, and MCP support

name: ai-berkshire
description: AI 时代的伯克希尔：基于 Claude Code / Codex 的价值投资研究框架。巴菲特·芒格·段永平·李录四大师方法论 + 多Agent并行研究。| AI-era Berkshire: a value investing research framework built for Claude Code / Codex. 4 masters' methodologies + multi-agent adversarial analysis.

curation.summary: AI Berkshire is a structured investment research framework that wraps value investing methodologies from Buffett, Munger, Duan Yongping, and Li Lu into Claude Code/Codex skills, enabling multi-agent adversarial analysis with forced conclusions, anti-bias mechanisms, and financial rigor verification. It aims to transform a single person with an LLM into a professional-grade investment research team by systematizing four masters' distinct analytical lenses into reproducible, structured workflows.
curation.whyNotable: The project stands out for its deeply domain-specific approach to prompt engineering—rather than generic AI chat, it encodes real investment discipline (forced buy/sell conclusions, 8-point red-line veto checklists, cross-source financial verification using Python Decimal) and claims verified real-money returns of 69% and 66% in consecutive years. The multi-master adversarial analysis concept, where different AI personas reach conflicting conclusions, is a genuinely novel application of multi-agent patterns to decision-making.
strengths: Exceptionally well-documented with clear real-world examples, comparison tables, and structured output samples that make the value proposition immediately apparent
Unique multi-master adversarial analysis design that creates productive intellectual tension between four distinct investment philosophies rather than a single consensus answer

Built-in anti-bias and rigor mechanisms (information richness grading, Munger-style inversion testing, financial calculation verification via Python Decimal, rapid veto checklists) that address real LLM failure modes
Strong community traction with 8K+ stars and bilingual (Chinese/English) documentation serving the large Chinese value investing community
limitations: No CI, tests, Docker, or release infrastructure—effectively a prompt/workflow collection without automated validation, making quality assurance dependent on manual review
Heavily coupled to specific LLM behaviors (Claude Code/Codex); prompt effectiveness may degrade silently with model updates, and there is no regression testing to detect this
Investment return claims (69%/66%) are presented as screenshots without independent verification or standardized benchmarking methodology, which undermines credibility for sophisticated users
useCases: Individual value investors conducting structured, multi-perspective equity research on stocks using AI assistance
Chinese and Hong Kong stock market analysts seeking a systematic framework to evaluate companies against proven investment master methodologies
Solo investors who want to replicate the analytical depth of a multi-person research team using Claude Code or Codex workflows
curation.targetAudience: Value investors (especially those following Buffett, Munger, Duan Yongping, or Li Lu philosophies) who are technically comfortable with Claude Code/Codex and want to systematize their investment research process
curation.oneLiner: A prompt-engineering framework that turns Claude Code into a multi-master adversarial investment research team with forced conclusions and anti-bias discipline

name: codebase-memory-mcp
description: High-performance code intelligence MCP server. Indexes codebases into a persistent knowledge graph — average repo in milliseconds. 158 languages, sub-ms queries, 99% fewer tokens. Single static binary, zero dependencies.

curation.summary: A high-performance code intelligence MCP server written in pure C that indexes entire codebases into persistent knowledge graphs using tree-sitter AST parsing and hybrid LSP semantic analysis. It supports 158 languages, processes repositories in milliseconds, and provides sub-millisecond query responses as a single static binary with zero dependencies.
curation.whyNotable: Exceptional performance (indexing the Linux kernel in 3 minutes) combined with extensive language support and seamless integration with 11 coding agents makes this a standout tool for enhancing AI coding workflows. The accompanying research paper validates its efficiency gains of 10× fewer tokens and 2.1× fewer tool calls compared to file-by-file exploration.
strengths: Blazing-fast indexing and querying (milliseconds for average repositories)
Comprehensive language support (158 languages) with enhanced semantic analysis for 9 major languages via hybrid LSP
Zero dependencies and single static binary for easy cross-platform deployment
Active development with 35 releases in 6 months and 62 contributors
Extensive test coverage (5,604+ tests) and CI/CD pipeline
limitations: Being a C binary may limit accessibility for contributions from developers unfamiliar with the language
Requires MCP-compatible AI agents to be useful, not a standalone tool
useCases: Providing structural code context to AI coding agents (Cursor, Aider, etc.)
Building persistent knowledge graphs of large codebases for analysis
Enabling fast code navigation and understanding in AI-assisted development workflows
curation.targetAudience: AI coding agent developers and users who need fast, comprehensive code intelligence integration
curation.oneLiner: The fastest code intelligence engine for AI agents, indexing entire repositories into knowledge graphs in milliseconds with zero dependencies.

name: design.md
description: A format specification for describing a visual identity to coding agents. DESIGN.md gives agents a persistent, structured understanding of a design system.

curation.summary: DESIGN.md is a format specification from Google Labs that lets developers describe visual design systems in a structured, AI-agent-readable format. It combines YAML front matter for machine-readable design tokens with markdown prose for human-readable design rationale, enabling AI coding agents to produce UIs that faithfully adhere to a design system.
curation.whyNotable: This project addresses a critical gap in the AI-assisted development workflow: how to give coding agents persistent, structured understanding of design systems rather than relying on ad-hoc prompting. Backed by Google Labs, it has achieved remarkable adoption (24k+ stars in just a few months) and includes practical CLI tooling for linting (including WCAG contrast checks) and diffing design system versions.
strengths: Elegant specification design that elegantly bridges machine-readable tokens with human-readable prose in a single file
Practical CLI tooling with lint (including WCAG contrast validation) and diff commands that output structured JSON for agent consumption
Massive community adoption (24k+ stars) indicating strong demand for structured design-to-agent communication
Well-structured specification with clear separation between normative tokens and contextual prose
Backed by Google Labs with active development (4 releases in 6 months, 17 contributors)
limitations: No automated test suite, which is a gap for a specification project that needs high reliability
Effectiveness depends on AI coding agents supporting and correctly interpreting the format — ecosystem adoption is still nascent
Alpha-stage specification may see breaking changes as the format matures
useCases: Providing AI coding assistants (Cursor, Copilot, etc.) with persistent design system context so generated UIs match brand guidelines
Version-controlling design systems with diffable, reviewable format changes across releases
Enforcing WCAG accessibility compliance and design token consistency via automated linting in CI pipelines
curation.targetAudience: Frontend developers and design engineers who use AI coding agents and need to ensure generated code adheres to established design systems; also design system teams maintaining cross-platform visual identity
curation.oneLiner: A Google-backed specification that gives AI coding agents a persistent, structured understanding of your design system through a single markdown file.

name: no-mistakes
description: git push no-mistakes

curation.summary: no-mistakes is a Go-based local git proxy that intercepts `git push` commands and runs an AI-driven validation pipeline (review, test, docs, lint) in an isolated worktree before forwarding branches to the real remote. It automatically applies safe fixes, escalates ambiguous issues to the developer, and opens clean PRs only after all checks pass.
curation.whyNotable: It creatively reimagines the git push workflow by inserting an AI-powered quality gate that works agent-agnostically with Claude, Codex, Copilot, and others, making it a unique intersection of developer tooling and AI agents that prevents mistakes before they reach the remote.
strengths: Agent-agnostic design supporting multiple AI coding assistants (Claude, Codex, Copilot, etc.)
Non-blocking architecture using disposable worktrees so developers' work is never disrupted
Seamless integration into existing git workflow — just push to a different remote and get clean PRs automatically
Rapid release cadence (77 releases in ~3 months) with cross-platform support and CI automation
limitations: No unit or integration tests listed in the repository, raising concerns about reliability and regression safety
Relies heavily on external AI agents for validation quality — behavior may be inconsistent across agent providers and API versions
useCases: Individual developers wanting to catch bugs, lint errors, and documentation gaps before opening PRs
Teams enforcing automated AI-powered code review gates in their git workflow
AI coding agent workflows where agents complete tasks and then gate their output through automated validation before human review
curation.targetAudience: Developers and teams using AI coding assistants who want to prevent sloppy or broken code from reaching their shared repositories
curation.oneLiner: An AI-powered git push proxy that gates every push behind automated review, test, and lint — so only clean code gets a PR.

name: page-agent
description: JavaScript in-page GUI agent. Control web interfaces with natural language.

curation.summary: Page Agent is an in-page JavaScript/TypeScript GUI agent by Alibaba that enables natural language control of web interfaces through text-based DOM manipulation, eliminating the need for screenshots, multimodal LLMs, browser extensions, or headless browsers. It supports bring-your-own LLM configurations and offers optional Chrome extension and MCP server integration for multi-page and external agent control scenarios.
curation.whyNotable: The text-based DOM manipulation approach is a clever and practical alternative to screenshot-based GUI agents, making it lightweight, dependency-free, and accessible without special permissions or multimodal models. With 20K+ stars, active development (33 releases in 6 months), and backing from Alibaba, it has strong momentum and real-world applicability for embedding AI copilots directly into web products with minimal integration effort.
strengths: Extremely lightweight integration — single script tag, no browser extensions, Python, or headless browser required

MCP server integration enables external agent orchestration and interoperability

Building accessibility tools that allow voice or text-based navigation of web applications

name: palmier-pro

curation.summary: Palmier Pro is an open-source, Swift-native macOS video editor designed with deep AI integration at its core. It combines traditional timeline-based video editing with built-in generative AI capabilities (Seedance, Kling, Nano Banana Pro) and exposes an MCP server allowing AI agents like Claude, Codex, and Cursor to directly manipulate video projects collaboratively.
curation.whyNotable: The project pioneers an MCP-first approach to creative software, enabling AI agents to co-edit video timelines directly — a novel paradigm for human-AI collaboration in content creation. Backed by Y Combinator S24 and built from scratch in Swift for macOS, it bridges the gap between professional video editing tools and the emerging AI agent ecosystem.
strengths: Deep MCP server integration allows multiple AI coding agents (Claude, Codex, Cursor, Claude Desktop) to interact with the video editor programmatically
Built natively in Swift for macOS with Apple Silicon optimization, providing strong performance for a video editing application
Extremely active development pace with 60 releases in 6 months, 15 contributors, and nearly 10K stars indicating strong community interest
Multilingual README support (16 languages) demonstrates attention to global developer and user accessibility
limitations: No automated test suite despite CI presence, raising concerns about regression safety and long-term code quality
Tightly coupled to macOS ecosystem (requires macOS 26 Tahoe on Apple Silicon), severely limiting cross-platform adoption
useCases: AI-assisted video editing where users can instruct an agent to generate, cut, or rearrange timeline content via natural language
Programmatic video production workflows where developers build automated video pipelines using MCP-connected agents

name: agent-native
description: A framework for building agent-native applications.

curation.summary: Agent-Native is an open-source TypeScript framework by BuilderIO for building agent-native applications where AI agents and UI share the same actions, state, and context. It defines a unified action layer that powers every app surface—UI, agent, HTTP, MCP, A2A, and CLI—along with a built-in agent runtime for chat, tools, skills, memory, jobs, and observability. The project ships with several template apps including screen recording (Clips), visual planning for coding agents (Plans), design prototyping, content editing, and slides generation.
curation.whyNotable: The project introduces a compelling architectural paradigm where every backend action is natively consumable by both human UI and AI agents through a single definition, eliminating the traditional separation between agent tooling and application logic. Backed by BuilderIO (known for Qwik and visual building tools), it has strong momentum with 100 releases in 6 months and 3,300+ stars, signaling significant community interest in agent-native app development.
strengths: Unified action model that seamlessly bridges UI, agents, HTTP, MCP, A2A, and CLI into a single codebase, reducing duplication and complexity
Rich set of production-ready template applications (Clips, Plans, Design, Content, Slides) demonstrating diverse real-world use cases
Built-in agent runtime with comprehensive features including chat, tools, skills, memory, jobs, observability, and handoffs
Backend agnostic design supporting Drizzle-compatible SQL databases and Nitro-compatible hosts
Very active development pace with 100 releases in 6 months and 38 contributors
limitations: No test suite present, raising concerns about long-term reliability and regression prevention
No examples directory and relatively minimal documentation beyond the README, making onboarding harder for complex use cases
Created very recently (March 2026), so the API surface and patterns may still be stabilizing
useCases: Building AI-powered productivity apps where agents can take the same actions as human users through a unified interface
Rapid prototyping of agent-native applications using template apps like design tools, content editors, and screen recording with AI-assisted debugging
Creating MCP and A2A-compatible services that expose application logic to external agents and ecosystems
curation.targetAudience: Full-stack TypeScript developers building AI-powered applications who want a unified architecture where agents and UI share the same action layer, particularly those integrating with MCP/A2A protocols
curation.oneLiner: A framework where every app action is natively shared between human UI and AI agents, enabling truly agent-native applications.

name: agent-toolkit-for-aws

curation.summary: The Agent Toolkit for AWS is an official, AWS-supported collection of MCP servers, skills, and plugins designed to give AI coding agents (such as Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Kiro) the tools and knowledge needed to build, deploy, and manage applications on AWS services. It provides specialized plugins covering core AWS infrastructure, AI agent development with Bedrock, data analytics, and DevSecOps workflows.
curation.whyNotable: This is the first official AWS-backed toolkit that bridges AI coding agents with AWS services via the Model Context Protocol (MCP), covering multiple popular AI agents simultaneously. Its official backing from AWS signals strong long-term support and alignment with the cloud provider's service ecosystem, making it a critical enabler for AI-assisted cloud development.
strengths: Official AWS support ensures alignment with AWS services, best practices, and long-term maintenance
Multi-agent compatibility (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Kiro) maximizes developer reach across popular AI coding tools
Domain-specific plugins (aws-core, aws-agents, aws-data-analytics, aws-agents-for-devsecops) provide focused, curated knowledge for different AWS workflows
Uses the standardized MCP protocol for interoperability with the growing MCP ecosystem
limitations: No tests, no Docker support, no examples, and zero releases indicate the project is still early-stage despite its GA badge
README documentation appears incomplete (cut off mid-JSON example) and lacks architecture details, contribution guidelines, and plugin development documentation
useCases: AI-assisted deployment and management of AWS infrastructure via CDK, CloudFormation, and serverless
Building AI agent applications on Amazon Bedrock with AgentCore using coding assistants
Automated DevSecOps workflows including incident investigation, vulnerability scanning, and penetration testing with AWS services
curation.targetAudience: AWS developers and cloud engineers who use AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Kiro) to build and manage AWS infrastructure and applications
curation.oneLiner: The first official AWS toolkit that gives AI coding agents native AWS knowledge and capabilities via MCP, bridging popular AI assistants with cloud infrastructure.

name: last30days-skill

curation.summary: last30days-skill is an AI agent plugin that performs parallel research across Reddit, X, YouTube, TikTok, Hacker News, Polymarket, and the web, then synthesizes results into a grounded brief ranked by real engagement metrics like upvotes, likes, and market odds. Designed as a universal skill for 50+ AI agent hosts including Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, and Gemini CLI, it bridges the walled gardens of social platforms that no single search engine or AI can access alone. The project has gained massive traction with nearly 48k stars and a vibrant contributor community.
curation.whyNotable: The project solves a genuinely unique problem: no single AI assistant can natively search Reddit, X, TikTok, YouTube, and Polymarket simultaneously. By packaging this as a portable agent skill with zero-config defaults, it enables a fundamentally new kind of research workflow—scoring information by what real people engage with rather than editorial algorithms or SEO rankings.
strengths: Bridges dozens of walled-garden platforms (Reddit, X, TikTok, YouTube, HN, Polymarket) into a single unified search, which no existing AI or search engine can do natively
Engagement-based scoring (upvotes, likes, real money on Polymarket) rather than editorial/SEO-based ranking provides genuinely novel signal
Exceptionally broad compatibility with 50+ AI agent hosts and easy zero-config installation, making it immediately usable across the ecosystem
limitations: Requires users to provide their own API keys and browser sessions for several platforms, creating setup friction for full functionality
Dependent on external platform APIs and scraping methods that are subject to breakage, rate limiting, or policy changes from X, TikTok, Reddit, etc.
useCases: Preparing for meetings by researching a person's recent activities, projects, and public engagement across social platforms
Tracking trending topics and public sentiment across multiple social and financial prediction markets simultaneously
Deep research on niche topics where the best signals live in community discussions (Reddit, HN) rather than traditional search results
curation.targetAudience: Developers and knowledge workers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot) who need multi-platform research capabilities; analysts and researchers who track trends across social media and prediction markets
curation.oneLiner: An agent skill that bridges Reddit, X, YouTube, TikTok, HN, and Polymarket into a single engagement-scored research search no other AI can provide alone

name: herdr
description: agent multiplexer that lives in your terminal.

curation.summary: herdr is a terminal multiplexer written in Rust that lets developers run and manage multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) in a single terminal. It provides real terminal views per agent, automatic agent state detection (blocked/working/done/idle), persistent sessions with detach/reattach support, and a mouse-native workspace/tab/pane system—essentially reimagining tmux for the era of AI coding agents.
curation.whyNotable: herdr solves an emerging pain point: as developers increasingly juggle multiple AI coding agents simultaneously, there's a need for centralized visibility and control. Its agent-awareness feature—automatically detecting whether an agent is blocked, working, or done without any configuration—combined with the familiar terminal multiplexer paradigm makes it a uniquely positioned tool that bridges traditional terminal workflows with the new AI-agent-driven development model.
strengths: Innovative agent-aware terminal multiplexer concept that fills a real gap between tmux (no agent awareness) and GUI agent managers (not terminal-native, platform-locked)
Impressive engineering: single ~10MB Rust binary, no dependencies, cross-platform, real PTY per agent so even TUI apps render correctly
Rapid and active development with 65 releases in 6 months, 34 contributors, and nearly 10K stars indicating strong community traction
limitations: License is marked as NOASSERTION, which creates legal ambiguity for adoption in enterprise or open-source-derivative contexts
Relatively young project (created March 2026) with 34 open issues and likely evolving API surface; no Docker support or example configurations yet
useCases: Developers running multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) simultaneously across different repositories and wanting unified visibility
Remote developers who need persistent agent sessions that survive SSH disconnects, accessible from any device including mobile
Teams orchestrating agent workflows via the socket API and CLI for automated multi-agent pipelines
curation.targetAudience: Power developers and AI-augmented engineers who run multiple coding agents in parallel and need a lightweight, terminal-native way to manage, monitor, and organize them
curation.oneLiner: A tmux reimagined for AI coding agents—run, watch, and orchestrate all your agents in one terminal with automatic state detection.

name: freellmapi
description: OpenAI-compatible proxy that stacks the free tiers of 16 LLM providers (~1.7B tokens/month) behind one /v1 endpoint — plus any custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Smart routing, automatic failover, encrypted keys. Personal experimentation only.

curation.summary: FreeLLMAPI is an OpenAI-compatible proxy that aggregates the free tiers of 18 LLM providers (161 models, ~1.7B tokens/month) behind a single /v1 endpoint. It features smart routing, automatic failover when providers are rate-limited, per-key usage tracking, and encrypted key storage, collapsing a fragmented landscape of free-tier APIs into one unified interface.
curation.whyNotable: The project solves a genuinely painful developer problem — managing dozens of free-tier LLM APIs with different SDKs, rate limits, and failure modes — by presenting them as a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Its rapid adoption (14.6K stars) demonstrates strong demand for unified LLM access, and its smart routing/failover system makes it practical to actually leverage all those free tiers together.
strengths: Aggregates 18 providers and 161 free models into a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint, dramatically simplifying multi-provider access
Smart routing with automatic failover, per-key usage tracking, and encrypted key storage
Strong community adoption with 14.6K stars, 39 contributors, Docker support, CI, and a live model catalog website
limitations: Explicitly labeled 'Personal experimentation only' — not designed or validated for production workloads
No test suite despite having CI infrastructure, which raises reliability concerns for the routing and failover logic
useCases: Personal hobby projects and LLM experimentation without incurring API costs
Prototyping and testing applications across diverse LLM models and providers
Developers wanting a single local API endpoint to swap between providers transparently
curation.targetAudience: Individual developers, hobbyists, and tinkerers who want to maximize free-tier LLM access for personal projects and experimentation
curation.oneLiner: An OpenAI-compatible proxy that stacks 18 free-tier LLM providers into one endpoint with smart routing and automatic failover — 1.7B free tokens/month.

curation.summary: SkillSpector is a security scanner for AI agent skills that detects vulnerabilities, malicious patterns, and security risks before installation. It supports multiple input formats, 68 vulnerability patterns across 17 categories, and offers two-stage analysis with optional LLM semantic evaluation.
curation.whyNotable: Addresses a critical and emerging security gap in the AI agent ecosystem, where skills often execute with implicit trust. Its comprehensive pattern coverage, live CVE lookups, and multi-format support make it a practical tool for both developers and security teams.

Multi-format input support (Git repos, URLs, zip files, directories, files)
Two-stage analysis combining fast static analysis with optional LLM semantic evaluation
Live vulnerability lookups via OSV.dev with offline fallback
Multiple output formats including SARIF for integration with security tools
limitations: No releases in the last 6 months, suggesting limited release cadence
No examples provided in the repository, which may hinder quick adoption
Relies on LLM for semantic analysis, which may introduce cost or latency
useCases: Pre-installation security scanning of AI agent skills from marketplaces or repositories
Continuous integration pipeline integration to vet skills before deployment
Security audits of existing agent skill collections in enterprise environments
curation.targetAudience: Security engineers, AI/ML developers, and platform teams responsible for deploying AI agents in production environments

Its comprehensive feature set covering tracing, evaluation, and prompt optimization in a single platform, combined with multi-language documentation and broad framework integrations, makes it a standout project in the LLMOps space.

No Docker deployment option or curated examples in the repository, potentially increasing onboarding friction

Automated evaluation and benchmarking of RAG systems, chatbots, and code assistants

Optimizing prompts and tool configurations through automated experimentation

Its design philosophy of keeping the system small and explicit while supporting flexible data generation and high-performance training makes it a practical and innovative tool for RL post-training.

Correctness-first infrastructure with explicit dataflow and debugging paths
Lightweight and opinionated design, avoiding heavy abstractions
Flexible data generation through custom interfaces and server-based engines

No Docker support, which may complicate deployment for some users

Scaling RL training loops for frontier model development
Integrating custom data generation and reward computation workflows
curation.targetAudience: AI researchers and engineers working on large-scale RL post-training of LLMs, particularly those using Megatron and SGLang.
curation.oneLiner: A battle-tested, lightweight RL post-training framework for scaling LLMs, powering frontier GLM models.

name: supervision
description: We write your reusable computer vision tools. 💜

curation.summary: Supervision is a widely-adopted open-source Python library providing reusable computer vision utilities including annotation tools, detection/segmentation helpers, dataset loaders, and video processing capabilities. It is model-agnostic, allowing users to plug in any classification, detection, or segmentation model, and offers a rich set of annotators, zone counting, and tracking features. Maintained by Roboflow, it has grown to over 46k stars with an active contributor community.
curation.whyNotable: Supervision has become the de facto standard utility library for computer vision developers, offering model-agnostic building blocks that dramatically reduce boilerplate code. Its massive adoption (46k+ stars, 168 contributors, frequent releases) and backing by Roboflow make it a cornerstone of the CV open-source ecosystem, bridging the gap between raw model outputs and polished visual applications.
strengths: Extremely mature and well-maintained with 168 contributors, CI, tests, codecov, and regular releases
Model-agnostic design works with any detection, classification, or segmentation framework (YOLO, Transformers, etc.)
Comprehensive feature set covering annotation, dataset loading (COCO, Pascal VOC), video processing, tracking, and zone counting
Excellent documentation with tutorials, Colab notebooks, HuggingFace demos, and Discord community
Low-code API that abstracts away complex CV post-processing tasks
limitations: Primarily a utility/toolkit library rather than a standalone end-to-end solution; requires external models for inference
Does not include Docker deployment configuration, which may limit some production containerized workflows
useCases: Annotating and visualizing object detection, segmentation, and classification results with rich built-in annotators
Loading and converting datasets between formats like COCO, Pascal VOC, and YOLO
Real-time video processing tasks including object counting in zones, tracking, and frame-by-frame analysis
curation.targetAudience: Computer vision engineers, ML practitioners, and researchers who need production-quality utility functions for model output visualization, dataset management, and video processing without reinventing common CV workflows

name: LMCache
description: LMCache: Supercharge Your LLM with the Fastest KV Cache Layer

curation.summary: LMCache is a high-performance KV cache management layer that accelerates LLM inference by optimizing how key-value caches are stored, shared, and reused across requests. It features a multiprocess architecture supporting multi-node P2P CPU memory sharing, integrates deeply with vLLM and NVIDIA Dynamo, and works across both NVIDIA and AMD GPU hardware. The project has demonstrated up to 10x inference performance improvements for MoE models.
curation.whyNotable: With nearly 10K stars, integration into the PyTorch Foundation ecosystem, partnerships with NVIDIA (Dynamo integration) and CoreWeave, and a novel multiprocess architecture that delivers dramatic speedups, LMCache has quickly established itself as a critical piece of LLM inference infrastructure. Its rapid release cadence (35 releases in 6 months) and 239 contributors signal strong momentum.
strengths: Deep integration with production inference stacks (vLLM, NVIDIA Dynamo) making adoption straightforward
Multi-hardware support spanning NVIDIA CUDA and AMD ROCm with demonstrated benchmarks on MI300X
Industry-backed with PyTorch Foundation membership, CoreWeave partnership, and NVIDIA collaboration
Novel multiprocess architecture enabling P2P CPU memory sharing across nodes for distributed KV cache
Extremely active development with 35 releases in 6 months and 239 contributors
limitations: 127 open issues suggest ongoing rough edges and evolving API surface
No Docker support indicated, potentially complicating deployment and reproducibility
useCases: Accelerating LLM inference in production serving environments by caching and reusing KV states
Multi-turn agentic workloads where repeated context reuse across turns reduces redundant computation
Multi-node LLM serving with distributed KV cache sharing to reduce inter-node memory bottlenecks
curation.targetAudience: LLM inference engineers and platform teams optimizing production serving infrastructure, ML infrastructure teams at companies deploying large language models at scale
curation.oneLiner: A blazing-fast KV cache management layer integrated with vLLM and NVIDIA Dynamo that delivers up to 10x inference speedups through multiprocess architecture and P2P memory sharing

Its token compression technology and auto-fallback routing across 231+ providers with 17 routing strategies represent a significant utility for developers who want to maximize free AI usage without vendor lock-in.
strengths: Massive provider coverage (236+ providers, 50+ free tiers) with a single unified endpoint, dramatically reducing integration complexity
Innovative RTK+Caveman stacked compression achieving 15-95% token savings, directly reducing costs for all connected applications

It provides keyword research, rank tracking, backlink analysis, site audits, and competitor insights, powered by the DataForSEO API. Notably, it exposes an MCP server and agent skills system, allowing AI agents like Claude Code and Hermes to directly interact with SEO data and execute structured SEO workflows.
curation.whyNotable: The project stands out by combining an accessible open-source SEO suite with native MCP/AI agent integration, making it one of the first SEO tools designed from the ground up for AI-agent-driven workflows. Its pay-as-you-go model via DataForSEO API removes expensive subscription barriers, and its agent skills system provides reusable, structured SEO task templates for autonomous agents.
strengths: First-class MCP server integration enabling AI agents to use SEO data directly, a differentiated approach compared to traditional SEO tools
Pay-as-you-go pricing model via DataForSEO API eliminates expensive subscriptions, making professional SEO accessible to individuals and small teams
Well-designed agent skills system (seo-coach, keyword-clustering, competitive-landscape, etc.) that provides structured, reusable AI workflows
Clean, focused UI with opinionated workflows rather than bloated feature sprawl, and self-hosting options via Cloudflare and Docker
limitations: No automated test suite despite having CI configured, raising concerns about reliability and regression prevention
Core SEO data pipeline is entirely dependent on a third-party API (DataForSEO), meaning outages or pricing changes could significantly impact the product
Small contributor base (5 contributors) and relatively new project (created Feb 2026) suggest limited long-term maintenance bandwidth
useCases: Small businesses and freelancers who need Semrush/Ahrefs-level SEO capabilities without enterprise pricing
Developers building AI-agent-driven SEO automation pipelines using MCP-compatible agents like Claude Code or Hermes
SEO professionals who want a self-hostable, customizable SEO platform they can extend with their own workflows and integrations
curation.targetAudience: Independent SEO practitioners, small business owners, and developers building AI-agent-based SEO workflows who want an affordable, open-source alternative to expensive commercial SEO suites

Its focus on LLM-ready output, agent/MCP integration, and handling 96% of the web with minimal configuration fills a crucial gap in the AI toolchain between raw web content and AI applications.
strengths: Massive community adoption (142K+ stars, 162 contributors) indicating strong ecosystem validation
LLM-optimized output formats (Markdown, structured JSON) reduce token costs for downstream AI applications
Handles complex web scraping challenges (JS rendering, proxies, rate limits) so developers don't have to
Available both as open-source self-hosted and a managed hosted service for flexibility
Active development with 4 releases in the last 6 months and clean API design supporting agent workflows
limitations: No automated test suite listed, raising concerns about regression safety and long-term maintainability
AGPL-3.0 license may be restrictive for commercial/proprietary integrations without a commercial license
useCases: Populating RAG pipelines with fresh, clean web content for LLM knowledge bases
Building AI agents that need to browse, search, and extract real-time web information
Converting web-hosted documents (PDFs, DOCX) into structured text for downstream NLP processing
Creating datasets for fine-tuning or evaluating LLMs from web sources

curation.whyNotable: HyperFrames is notable for its unique approach of using HTML as a video authoring language, combined with deep integration for AI agents via 19 specialized skills. Its rapid release cadence (100 releases in 6 months) and strong community adoption (32k stars) signal a fast-moving, well-funded project that could redefine programmatic video generation.
strengths: Novel concept of using HTML/CSS for deterministic video rendering
Extensive agent skill system (19 skills) for AI-driven video creation
Excellent documentation and quickstart guides with a playground and showcase

name: crawlee
description: Crawlee—A web scraping and browser automation library for Node.js to build reliable crawlers. In JavaScript and TypeScript. Extract data for AI, LLMs, RAG, or GPTs. Download HTML, PDF, JPG, PNG, and other files from websites. Works with Puppeteer, Playwright, Cheerio, JSDOM, and raw HTTP. Both headful and headless mode. With proxy rotation.

curation.summary: Crawlee is a comprehensive web scraping and browser automation library for Node.js/TypeScript that provides an end-to-end solution for building reliable web crawlers. It supports multiple scraping backends including Puppeteer, Playwright, Cheerio, JSDOM, and raw HTTP, with built-in proxy rotation and human-like browsing patterns to avoid bot detection. The library is maintained by Apify and positions itself as a tool for extracting web data for AI/LLM/RAG applications.
curation.whyNotable: Crawlee stands out as one of the most mature and full-featured web scraping frameworks in the Node.js ecosystem, with 24k+ stars, 133 contributors, and strong production adoption through its parent company Apify. Its unified API across multiple scraping engines (browser-based and HTTP-based) with automatic proxy rotation and anti-detection capabilities make it a go-to solution for large-scale data collection pipelines feeding AI systems.
strengths: Supports multiple scraping backends (Puppeteer, Playwright, Cheerio, JSDOM, HTTP) through a unified API
Built-in proxy rotation and anti-bot-detection features for reliable scraping at scale
Well-maintained with active development, CI/CD, comprehensive documentation site, and strong community (24k+ stars, Discord)
TypeScript-first with clean modular architecture (@crawlee/core, per-adapter packages)
Handles full crawling lifecycle including request queuing, session management, and data storage
limitations: No Docker support indicated, which may limit containerized deployment scenarios
No built-in examples in the repository despite being a library—users must visit external docs
Node.js/TypeScript only for the main repo (Python version is separate), limiting cross-language ecosystem
useCases: Collecting web data at scale to feed LLM training pipelines or RAG knowledge bases
Building automated monitoring and scraping pipelines for e-commerce, news, or research data
Browser automation tasks requiring reliable anti-detection and proxy management
curation.targetAudience: Web scraping developers, data engineers building AI/ML data pipelines, and teams needing reliable large-scale web data extraction
curation.oneLiner: A battle-tested, multi-backend web scraping framework with built-in anti-detection that bridges the gap between raw data collection and AI/LLM data pipelines.

name: CopilotKit
description: The Frontend Stack for Agents & Generative UI. React, Angular, Mobile, Slack, and more.  Makers of the AG-UI Protocol

curation.summary: CopilotKit is an open-source frontend SDK and framework for building agent-native applications with generative UI, shared state, and human-in-the-loop workflows across React, Angular, Vue, React Native, and beyond. The project is also the creator of the AG-UI Protocol, an open standard for agent-to-UI communication adopted by major companies including Google, LangChain, AWS, and Microsoft.
curation.whyNotable: CopilotKit is notable for creating the AG-UI Protocol — an emerging open standard for agentic UI communication adopted by industry leaders — and for evolving from a simple React copilot library into a multi-platform agentic framework that bridges the gap between LLM backends and rich frontend experiences across web, mobile, and Slack.
strengths: Multi-platform support spanning React, Angular, Vue, React Native, and Slack from a single agent backend
Creator of the AG-UI Protocol, an open standard adopted by Google, LangChain, AWS, Microsoft, and other major players

MIT licensed with extensive documentation site and examples

331 open issues suggest potential maintenance backlog and possible stability concerns for production use

Creating agent-native applications with streaming generative UI components and real-time state sharing

curation.oneLiner: The leading open-source frontend framework for building agent-native applications with generative UI, powered by the widely-adopted AG-UI Protocol.

name: hivemind
description: Hivemind turns your traces into reusable skills across agents

curation.summary: Hivemind is a cloud-backed shared memory system for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) that captures agent session traces, mines them for repeated patterns, and codifies discovered patterns into reusable skills that propagate across all agents on a team. Built on Deeplake by Activeloop (YC-backed), it enables collective learning where one agent's breakthroughs benefit the entire team's agents.
curation.whyNotable: Hivemind moves beyond simple agent memory by actively extracting reusable skills from traces—turning individual agent experiences into team-wide capabilities. Its benchmark results on LoCoMo (25% cheaper, 1.7× fewer tokens, 31% fewer turns) demonstrate real efficiency gains, and the 100 releases in 3 months signal exceptionally rapid iteration.
strengths: Novel trace-to-skill pipeline that goes beyond simple RAG/memory by actively codifying patterns into reusable SKILL.md artifacts
Strong benchmarking against public LoCoMo long-context memory benchmark with measurable cost and token savings
Extremely active development with 100 releases in ~3 months, CI, tests, and growing community (1,400+ stars)
Multi-agent support across Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenClaw, and others—agent-agnostic shared brain
limitations: No Docker support or examples provided, which may hinder onboarding and self-hosted adoption
Tightly coupled to Deeplake as the backing store, creating vendor dependency for the core functionality
useCases: Engineering teams wanting their AI coding agents to collectively learn from each other's problem-solving traces
Organizations looking to reduce AI coding agent token costs and session length through shared learned patterns
Teams standardizing best practices by letting agents discover and propagate repeated successful workflows
curation.targetAudience: Engineering teams and organizations using AI coding agents (Cursor, Claude Code, Codex) who want collective agent intelligence and cost reduction
curation.oneLiner: A shared brain for AI coding agents that mines session traces into reusable skills, enabling collective team-wide agent learning.

It offers flexible storage options including local, Git, and cloud with end-to-end encryption, catering to both individual and collaborative workflows.
curation.whyNotable: It stands out as a comprehensive, open-source alternative to commercial API clients with strong community backing, offering features like E2EE and multiple sync options for enhanced security and flexibility in API development.
strengths: Supports multiple protocols (REST, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSockets, SSE) for broad API coverage.
Flexible storage options (local, Git, cloud with E2EE) provide security and collaboration versatility.
Cross-platform availability and open-source licensing under Apache-2.0 foster accessibility and community contributions.
limitations: Lacks AI-specific features, making it less relevant to AI-focused categories or workflows.
High number of open issues (729) and absence of mentioned tests may indicate stability or maintenance challenges.

Building and running API tests in CI/CD pipelines using the native CLI.

name: compound-engineering-plugin
description: Official Compound Engineering plugin for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and more

curation.summary: Compound Engineering is an official plugin for AI coding tools like Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor that implements a structured 6-step engineering workflow—brainstorm, plan, work, simplify, review, and compound—to ensure each unit of engineering work makes subsequent work easier. It encodes learnings into reusable documentation so AI agents accumulate context over time rather than rediscovering the same lessons. The project provides slash-command-based skills that orchestrate multi-agent planning, execution, and review workflows.
curation.whyNotable: It operationalizes a compelling 'compound engineering' philosophy where AI-assisted development gets progressively smarter by capturing and reusing institutional knowledge. With 22K+ stars in under a year and 100 releases in 6 months, it demonstrates strong community traction and active development for a novel approach to AI-native software engineering workflows.
strengths: Well-articulated philosophy that distinguishes it from generic AI coding tools by focusing on knowledge compounding across sessions
Structured multi-step workflow with dedicated slash commands for brainstorming, planning, execution, review, and learning capture
Very active development cadence with 100 releases in 6 months, 69 contributors, and CI/tests in place
Integrates across multiple AI coding tools (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) rather than being locked to one platform
limitations: Tightly coupled to specific AI coding tools which may limit adoption as the landscape evolves
Relies heavily on users following the full workflow discipline—skipping steps breaks the compounding benefit
No example projects or Docker-based quickstart makes initial evaluation harder for new users
useCases: Engineering teams wanting structured AI-assisted development workflows that retain institutional knowledge
Solo developers using AI coding assistants who want to avoid repeating context setup across sessions
Organizations looking to standardize how AI agents plan, execute, and review code changes
curation.targetAudience: Software engineers and engineering teams using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex) who want to build compounding knowledge into their development process rather than starting fresh each session
curation.oneLiner: A plugin that makes AI coding assistants get smarter over time by capturing and reusing engineering knowledge across sessions.

name: cypress
description: Fast, easy and reliable testing for anything that runs in a browser.

curation.summary: Cypress is a fast, easy-to-use, and reliable end-to-end testing framework for web applications. It runs in the browser and provides features like time travel debugging, automatic waiting, and real-time reloads to simplify test authoring and execution.
curation.whyNotable: Cypress revolutionized web testing by moving tests into the browser for consistent execution, offering an exceptional developer experience with its intuitive API and rich debugging capabilities. It's widely adopted by large companies and has a massive community, making it an industry standard for browser testing.
strengths: Excellent developer experience with real-time reloading and time travel debugging
Automatic waiting eliminates flaky tests
Rich ecosystem with plugins for modern frameworks and CI/CD integration
limitations: Limited to browser-based testing (no native mobile app support)
Cannot drive two browsers simultaneously in a single test
useCases: End-to-end testing of web applications

name: svelte
description: web development for the rest of us

It eliminates the virtual DOM by converting components into optimized imperative code at build time, making web development more accessible.
curation.whyNotable: Svelte's innovative compile-time approach results in smaller bundle sizes and superior runtime performance compared to traditional virtual DOM frameworks, driving its widespread adoption and active community development.
strengths: Compiler-based architecture for optimal performance and reduced runtime overhead
Easy to learn with intuitive syntax and a gentle learning curve
Active development, frequent releases, and strong community support
limitations: Not focused on AI or ML development, limiting relevance to the AI ecosystem
Requires build tools and may have a less extensive plugin ecosystem compared to some older frameworks

Prototyping and creating efficient front-end projects for general web development
curation.targetAudience: Web developers and front-end engineers seeking a performant, easy-to-use framework for building modern web applications
curation.oneLiner: A compiler that turns declarative components into efficient JavaScript for building web apps without a virtual DOM, emphasizing simplicity and performance.

name: impeccable
description: The design language that makes your AI harness better at design.

curation.summary: Impeccable is a design guidance system for AI coding agents that combats the generic, template-driven look of AI-generated frontends. It provides a single installable skill with 23 commands (like polish, audit, critique, shape) and 45 deterministic detector rules that enforce design quality without requiring LLM calls or API keys. The tool establishes project-specific design context through init and enables live browser iteration for visual refinement.
curation.whyNotable: It directly addresses a widespread problem in AI-assisted development—the homogenization of UI design where every AI-generated project looks like the same SaaS template. By combining deterministic rule-based checks with LLM critique capabilities, it offers a practical bridge between automated quality assurance and creative design guidance, filling a gap that existing AI coding tools haven't solved.
strengths: Addresses a genuine, widely-recognized pain point in AI-generated UIs with a structured solution rather than ad-hoc prompting
Hybrid approach of 45 deterministic rules (no API key needed) plus LLM critique checks provides both speed and depth
Strong adoption signals with 42K+ stars, 31 contributors, and 27 releases in 6 months indicating active maintenance
limitations: Narrowly focused on frontend/UI design, limiting applicability to non-visual AI development workflows
Dependent on the quality and consistency of the AI coding agent it's integrated with, creating a coupling to specific tools
useCases: Improving design quality of AI-generated React/Vue/frontend projects during development
Running automated design audits and accessibility checks on AI-produced code
Establishing and enforcing consistent design systems and brand guidelines in AI-assisted workflows
curation.targetAudience: Frontend developers and designers using AI coding agents (like Cursor, Claude, Copilot) who want to avoid generic-looking AI-generated UIs
curation.oneLiner: A design guidance skill for AI coding agents that eliminates the generic 'AI look' with 45 deterministic rules and 23 design commands

curation.whyNotable: Windmill stands out for its combination of a high-performance workflow engine (claimed 13x faster than Airflow), multi-language script support, and auto-generated UIs, all in a self-hostable package. Its rapid release cadence (100 releases in 6 months) and strong community engagement (17k stars, 165 contributors) indicate active development and adoption.

Auto-generated UIs from scripts and low-code app builder

name: iroh
description: IP addresses break, dial keys instead. A library that adds QUIC + NAT Traversal to your apps.

curation.summary: Iroh is a Rust library that provides an API for peer-to-peer networking by dialing public keys instead of IP addresses, using QUIC for connections and NAT traversal with hole-punching and relay servers for reliability. It supports composable protocols like blob transfer, gossip networks, and key-value stores for scalable distributed applications.
curation.whyNotable: Notable for its innovative approach to simplifying peer-to-peer networking with QUIC and automatic NAT traversal, enabling direct connections in challenging network environments, backed by a growing community and active development.
strengths: Innovative public key dialing for simplified P2P connections
Built on QUIC for high-performance, authenticated encryption and stream multiplexing
Supports NAT traversal and hole-punching with relay server fallbacks
Composable protocols (e.g., iroh-blobs, iroh-gossip) for versatile use cases
limitations: Not directly related to AI, limiting its fit in AI-focused categories
Rust-specific, which may restrict adoption to the Rust ecosystem
Metadata indicates lack of formal tests, potentially raising production readiness concerns
useCases: Peer-to-peer file sharing and content distribution using iroh-blobs
Real-time communication systems and pub-sub networks with iroh-gossip
Distributed applications requiring direct, low-latency connections across NATs
curation.targetAudience: Rust developers building distributed systems, peer-to-peer applications, or real-time networking tools
curation.oneLiner: A Rust library enabling direct peer-to-peer connections by dialing public keys with QUIC and NAT traversal.

name: cc-switch

name: agentgateway
description: Next Generation Agentic Proxy for AI Agents and MCP servers

curation.summary: Agentgateway is an open-source, Rust-based proxy/gateway purpose-built for agentic AI communication, providing a unified infrastructure layer for LLM routing, MCP tool federation, and A2A agent-to-agent communication. It delivers drop-in security, observability, and governance across agent-to-LLM, agent-to-tool, and agent-to-agent interactions through AI-native protocols. The project positions itself as the first complete connectivity solution for the emerging agentic AI ecosystem.
curation.whyNotable: Agentgateway addresses a critical and underserved infrastructure gap — the lack of standardized, secure, and observable communication pathways for AI agents. By unifying LLM gateway, MCP gateway, and A2A gateway capabilities into a single Rust-based proxy, it tackles the 'plumbing' problem that every multi-agent or tool-augmented LLM system must solve. Its rapid growth (3.6k stars in ~4 months) and 170 contributors signal strong ecosystem demand, and its alignment with emerging standards (MCP, A2A) positions it as foundational infrastructure for the agentic AI era.

name: fff

curation.summary: fff is a high-performance file search SDK written in Rust that provides typo-resistant path and content search with frecency-ranked file access for AI agents, code editors, and CLI tools. Originally a beloved Neovim plugin, it evolved into a multi-language library (Rust, C, Python, Node.js, Bun) with an MCP server integration for AI coding agents. It claims to be significantly faster than ripgrep and fzf in long-running processes.

Its MCP server integration makes it immediately useful for Claude Code, Cursor, and other AI coding assistants, while its adoption by nushell and opencode validates its real-world utility. The frecency-based ranking and auto-fuzzy fallback demonstrate thoughtful engineering beyond raw speed.

curation.summary: mistral.rs is a high-performance LLM inference engine written in Rust, designed for fast and flexible model serving with OpenAI and Anthropic API compatibility. It supports a wide range of models including multimodal variants (text, image, video, audio), and includes features like paged attention, prefix caching, CUDA optimizations, and an integrated agentic runtime with web search, code execution, and custom tool hooks.

Its combination of cutting-edge CUDA optimizations (CUDA graphs, FlashInfer), multimodal support (including DiffusionGemma), and a built-in agentic runtime makes it one of the most feature-complete open-source inference engines available, with very active development (31 releases in 6 months).

Broad API compatibility with both OpenAI and Anthropic endpoints for easy integration

name: 3x-ui

curation.summary: 3X-UI is an advanced open-source web control panel for managing Xray-core proxy and VPN servers, supporting a wide array of protocols including VLESS, VMess, Trojan, Shadowsocks, WireGuard, Hysteria2, and more. It is a feature-rich fork of the original X-UI project, offering per-client traffic accounting, multi-node management, modern transport layers (WebSocket, gRPC, XHTTP, REALITY), and a polished multi-language web interface. Written in Go, it targets individuals looking to deploy and manage proxy infrastructure from a single VPS or across multiple servers.
curation.whyNotable: With over 42,000 stars and a very active contributor community, 3X-UI is one of the most popular proxy management panels in the open-source ecosystem. Its broad protocol support, intuitive web UI, per-client management features, and frequent release cadence (26 releases in 6 months) make it a standout tool in the proxy/VPN self-hosting space.
strengths: Exceptionally broad protocol support covering VLESS, VMess, Trojan, Shadowsocks, WireGuard, Hysteria2, HTTP, SOCKS, and tunneling — all managed from a single panel

Very active development with 228 contributors, frequent releases, CI/CD pipeline, Docker support, and multi-language documentation (8+ languages)
limitations: No automated tests present, which raises concerns about regression safety and long-term code quality despite the high release frequency
Explicitly disclaimed as intended for personal use only — not designed for or suitable for production/enterprise proxy infrastructure
useCases: Individual users self-hosting proxy/VPN services on a VPS for personal privacy and internet freedom
Managing multi-node proxy deployments with per-user traffic accounting and subscription distribution
Deploying modern proxy protocols (REALITY, XTLS, Hysteria2) with minimal configuration through a web UI

name: beads

curation.summary: Beads is a distributed graph issue tracker for AI agents, providing persistent structured memory via a Dolt-powered SQL database. It replaces markdown plans with dependency-aware task tracking, enabling coding agents to maintain context over long-horizon tasks.
curation.whyNotable: Beads addresses a critical pain point for AI coding agents—context loss and messy markdown planning—by offering a version-controlled, agent-optimized issue tracker with built-in branching and sync. Its rapid adoption (25k stars, 399 contributors) and frequent releases (34 in 6 months) signal strong community interest and active development.
strengths: Dolt-powered version control with cell-level merge and branching
Agent-optimized JSON output and dependency tracking
Zero-conflict hash-based IDs for distributed collaboration
Extensive agent/IDE integrations (Claude, Codex, Cursor, etc.)
Strong documentation and quick-start guides
limitations: Relatively new project (created Oct 2025) with 285 open issues
No Docker support, limiting containerized deployments
Learning curve for agents unfamiliar with graph-based task tracking
useCases: Persistent memory for AI coding agents during long development sessions
Distributed issue tracking for multi-agent systems
Structured task management in AI-assisted software projects
curation.targetAudience: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (e.g., Claude Code, Codex CLI) who need reliable, version-controlled task tracking and context persistence.
curation.oneLiner: A distributed graph issue tracker that gives AI coding agents persistent, version-controlled memory.

name: prometheus
description: The Prometheus monitoring system and time series database.

curation.summary: Prometheus is a CNCF graduated, open-source systems and service monitoring system and time series database that collects metrics from configured targets, evaluates rule expressions, displays results, and triggers alerts. It features a multi-dimensional data model, the powerful PromQL query language, an HTTP pull-based collection model, and autonomous single-server node architecture. Originally created in 2012, it has become the de facto standard for cloud-native monitoring.
curation.whyNotable: Prometheus is the industry standard for cloud-native monitoring, having pioneered the pull-based metrics collection model and the multi-dimensional time series data approach that influenced an entire ecosystem of monitoring tools. Its CNCF graduation, massive adoption across the industry, and the PromQL query language have made it a foundational piece of modern infrastructure.
strengths: Battle-tested CNCF graduated project with massive real-world adoption and 64k+ GitHub stars
Innovative pull-based monitoring model with powerful PromQL query language and multi-dimensional data model
Extensive ecosystem with 1400+ contributors, regular releases, comprehensive CI/CD, and robust security tooling (OSS-Fuzz, OpenSSF Scorecard, govulncheck)
limitations: Not an AI/ML project — falls outside the AI category taxonomy entirely
Long-term storage requires external solutions (Thanos, Cortex) as native storage is limited to local disk
useCases: Infrastructure and application metrics monitoring in cloud-native environments
Alerting and alert management for production systems
Time series data collection and querying for operational dashboards
curation.targetAudience: DevOps engineers, SREs, platform engineers, and organizations running cloud-native infrastructure
curation.oneLiner: The CNCF-graduated, industry-standard monitoring system and time series database that pioneered pull-based metrics collection and PromQL.

name: ainovel-cli
description: ✨多agent实现全自动AI小说生成

curation.summary: ainovel-cli is a Go-based multi-agent AI novel generation engine that autonomously produces full-length novels from a single prompt. A Coordinator agent orchestrates Architect, Writer, and Editor sub-agents through a long-loop LLM-driven pipeline, featuring dual-layer rolling planning, step-level checkpoint recovery, and a seven-dimension quality review system capable of handling 500+ chapter novels.
curation.whyNotable: The project's 'single prompt, full book' architecture is genuinely innovative — it collapses traditional multi-step creative workflows into one autonomous long-loop with intelligent checkpoint recovery. The dual-layer rolling planning (only planning near-term chapters in detail while maintaining high-level arc structure) solves the notorious long-context coherence problem in AI-generated fiction at scale.
strengths: Sophisticated multi-agent architecture with clear role separation (Coordinator/Architect/Writer/Editor) and well-defined tool boundaries
Novel dual-layer rolling planning system that avoids empty long-range planning while maintaining narrative coherence across 500+ chapters
Robust step-level checkpoint recovery ensuring crash resilience and the ability to resume exactly where execution left off
limitations: No automated tests despite having CI, which undermines confidence in correctness and regression safety
README is entirely in Chinese with no English documentation, limiting accessibility for the broader open-source community
useCases: Autonomously generating full-length novels (100K+ words) from a single-sentence creative brief
Long-form creative writing research exploring multi-agent collaboration patterns for narrative coherence
Rapid prototyping of fiction outlines and drafts for authors seeking AI-assisted ideation
curation.targetAudience: AI researchers studying multi-agent creative systems, indie novelists exploring AI-assisted writing, and Go developers interested in LLM orchestration patterns
curation.oneLiner: A single-prompt multi-agent engine that autonomously writes entire novels with rolling planning, checkpoint recovery, and seven-dimension quality review

Cloud-native design with multi-tenancy, replication, and RBAC for production deployments

Chatbots and conversational AI with hybrid retrieval

name: LocalAI
description: LocalAI is the open-source AI engine. Run any model - LLMs, vision, voice, image, video - on any hardware. No GPU required.

curation.summary: LocalAI is a comprehensive open-source AI engine written in Go that enables running LLMs, vision models, voice/TTS, image generation, and video models locally on any hardware including CPU-only setups. It provides OpenAI, Anthropic, and ElevenLabs API-compatible endpoints by wrapping best-in-class backends (llama.cpp, vLLM, whisper.cpp, Stable Diffusion, MLX, etc.) in a composable, on-demand architecture. The project emphasizes privacy, hardware flexibility, and extensibility with built-in agent support, RAG, MCP, and multi-user access controls.
curation.whyNotable: LocalAI stands out as the most comprehensive open-source local AI engine, combining nearly every modality (text, vision, voice, image, video) under a single API surface with drop-in compatibility for OpenAI/Anthropic/ElevenLabs APIs. Its composable backend architecture — pulling only what's needed — combined with over 47K stars, 214 contributors, and 34 releases in 6 months signals an extremely active, community-driven project that democratizes AI inference without cloud dependencies or GPU requirements.
strengths: Extraordinary breadth of modality support — LLMs, vision, voice, image generation, and video behind a single unified API
Composable architecture with backends pulled on demand, avoiding bloated installations while supporting llama.cpp, vLLM, whisper.cpp, Stable Diffusion, MLX, and more
Exceptional hardware flexibility spanning NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, Apple Silicon, Vulkan, and CPU-only deployments
Drop-in API compatibility with OpenAI, Anthropic, and ElevenLabs APIs, making migration trivial
Extremely active development with 34 releases in 6 months, 214 contributors, and 47K+ stars
Built-in agent framework with tool use, RAG, MCP protocol support, and skills
limitations: Performance on CPU-only hardware, while functional, can be significantly slower than GPU-accelerated alternatives for larger models
With many backends and modalities, configuration complexity can grow substantially for advanced use cases, and troubleshooting across diverse backends can be challenging
Open issues (133) suggest some rough edges or unresolved bugs across the wide surface area of supported backends
useCases: Running LLMs locally for privacy-sensitive applications (healthcare, legal, enterprise) without sending data to cloud providers
Building local AI-powered applications with OpenAI-compatible API endpoints for development and prototyping
Deploying multi-modal AI services (chat, image generation, TTS, vision) on edge devices or resource-constrained hardware
Creating autonomous AI agents with tool use and RAG capabilities running entirely on-premises
Self-hosted alternative to OpenAI/Azure AI services for organizations requiring data sovereignty
curation.targetAudience: Developers, DevOps engineers, and organizations wanting to run AI models locally for privacy, cost control, or hardware flexibility — from hobbyists running models on laptops to enterprises deploying on-premises AI infrastructure
curation.oneLiner: The Swiss Army knife of local AI inference — every modality, every hardware, one OpenAI-compatible API.

It underwent a major 1.0 rewrite from TypeScript to Go, delivering a high-performance CLI/TUI coding assistant with tool-use capabilities.
curation.whyNotable: With 25K+ stars, 94 contributors, and 69 releases in 6 months, this is one of the fastest-growing AI coding agents in the terminal space. Its unique engineering around DeepSeek's prefix-cache architecture and the ambitious TypeScript-to-Go rewrite demonstrate serious investment in performance and developer experience.
strengths: DeepSeek-native optimization with prefix-cache stability for persistent sessions
Ground-up Go rewrite delivering native binary performance over the TypeScript v1

name: restic

curation.summary: Restic is a fast, secure, and efficient open-source backup program that supports multiple storage backends and features deduplication and encryption by default. It provides cross-platform support and a simple command-line interface for creating, restoring, and managing backups with strong focus on data integrity.
curation.whyNotable: Restic stands out for its cryptographically verified backup architecture, content-addressable storage with deduplication, and support for numerous cloud and local storage backends. Its design prioritizes security and efficiency, making it a reliable tool for both personal and enterprise backup strategies.
strengths: Strong encryption and security-by-design philosophy
Highly efficient with automatic deduplication
Supports a wide variety of storage backends (S3, Azure, GCS, SFTP, local, etc.)
limitations: Not an AI/ML tool, so irrelevant to AI development workflows
No built-in AI features or integrations with machine learning ecosystems
useCases: Secure system and file backups to local or cloud storage
Automated backup workflows for servers and personal computers
Data deduplication for storage efficiency in backup operations

name: whatsmeow
